description: Completed three-way business combination of Owl Rock Capital Group (direct lending), Dyal Capital Partners (GP stakes), and Altimeter Growth Corp. (SPAC) to form Blue Owl Capital Inc., listing on NYSE under ticker OWL.
stated_rationale: Create a differentiated permanent-capital-oriented alternative asset manager combining Owl Rock's direct lending origination engine (~$30B AUM at close) with Dyal's GP stakes franchise (~$20B AUM), leveraging Altimeter SPAC capital to accelerate growth without dilutive traditional IPO pricing.
observable_metric_impact: Combined entity launched with ~$52B in AUM at closing. By Q4 2022, AUM reached ~$130B. Permanent capital represented approximately 85-90% of fee-paying AUM from inception, establishing the highest permanent-capital ratio among publicly listed alt managers.
op_prereq_summary: Required SEC/FINRA approvals for the SPAC de-SPAC transaction, integration of Owl Rock and Dyal back-office and compliance infrastructure, and reconciliation of two distinct LP bases with different fee structures and reporting cadences.

description: Launched and closed GP Strategic Capital Fund V (formerly Dyal Capital Partners V), raising approximately $12.9B to acquire minority stakes in established alternative asset managers.
stated_rationale: Extend the GP stakes franchise that was Dyal's founding strategy — providing permanent, non-dilutive capital to GPs seeking liquidity or growth financing without ceding control. Fund V targeted larger GPs and expanded into structured equity and preferred instruments alongside traditional minority stakes.
observable_metric_impact: GP Strategic Capital Fund V final close at ~$12.9B made it one of the largest GP-stakes vehicles ever raised. The fund deployed into stakes in managers including IPI Partners, Vista Equity, and others. GP stakes AUM grew from ~$20B at Blue Owl formation to ~$55B by end of 2023, with ~50 GP relationships.
op_prereq_summary: Required seasoned GP-stakes deal team with proprietary relationships, sophisticated valuation framework for illiquid GP equity, and LP confidence in the Dyal track record under the new Blue Owl corporate structure.

description: Acquired Oak Street Real Estate Capital, a net-lease-focused real estate platform, for approximately $950M in cash and stock, establishing Blue Owl Real Estate as a third major product pillar alongside Credit and GP Strategic Capital.
stated_rationale: Enter the ~$14T commercial real estate market through a differentiated net-lease strategy that generates contractual, inflation-linked cash flows aligned with Blue Owl's permanent-capital philosophy. Oak Street's sale-leaseback model produces long-duration, bond-like income streams attractive to insurance and institutional allocators.
observable_metric_impact: Added ~$14B in real estate AUM at close, bringing total Blue Owl AUM to ~$145B. By end of 2024, real estate AUM had grown to approximately $30B. Blue Owl Real Estate became the firm's fastest-growing segment, contributing meaningful diversification to the fee base beyond credit and GP stakes.
op_prereq_summary: Required integration of Oak Street's property-level operations and asset management teams, harmonization of fund structures under Blue Owl's permanent-capital framework, and cross-selling Oak Street products to Blue Owl's existing insurance and institutional LP relationships.

description: Acquired Kuvare Asset Management, an insurance asset management platform managing approximately $20B in general-account assets for insurance company clients, deepening Blue Owl's insurance-channel distribution capabilities.
stated_rationale: Secure captive insurance distribution channel and embedded asset management relationships. Kuvare provides permanent capital relationships with insurance balance sheets, accelerating Blue Owl's strategy of becoming a preferred private-credit and real-estate allocation partner for the insurance industry — mirroring the Apollo-Athene model at smaller scale.
observable_metric_impact: Added ~$20B in insurance-managed AUM. Total Blue Owl AUM exceeded $230B following completion. The acquisition strengthened Blue Owl's insurance channel, which by mid-2025 represented an estimated 25-30% of total inflows, positioning the firm alongside Apollo and Ares in the insurance-asset-management convergence trend.
op_prereq_summary: Required state insurance regulatory approvals across multiple jurisdictions, integration of Kuvare's investment-management-agreement structures with Blue Owl's existing fund administration, and retention of Kuvare's insurance-relationship management team to maintain client continuity.
